options.text: The mass of the gas at a given temperature compared with the mass of an equal volume of gas at the same temperature at a different given temperature

The phenomenon where the stabilty of two stratified layers of liquid of differing relative density distributed resulting in a spomtaneous rapid mixing of the layers accompanied in the case of liquefied gases, by violent vapour evolution
The pressure at which a vapour is in equilibrium with its liquid at a specific temperature
Heat energy given to or taken from a substance which raises or lowers its temperature
